lines.text: Then comes my fit again: I had else been perfect,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Whole as the marble, founded as the rock,

lines.speaker: MACBETH

lines.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
lines.text: The least a death to nature.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Thanks for that:

lines.text: Hath nature that in time will venom breed,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: No teeth for the present. Get thee gone: to-morrow

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: My royal lord,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: You do not give the cheer: the feast is sold

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: ’Tis given with welcome: to feed were best at home;

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Sweet remembrancer!

lines.text: Now, good digestion wait on appetite,

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: May’t please your highness sit. The Ghost of Banquo enters, and sits in Macbeth’s place.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Here had we now our country’s honour roof’d,

lines.text: Than pity for mischance!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: His absence, sir,

lines.text: Lays blame upon his promise. Please’t your highness

lines.text: To grace us with your royal company.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The table’s full.

lines.text: Here is a place reserved, sir.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Here, my good lord. What is’t that moves your highness?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Which of you have done this?

lines.text: What, my good lord?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Thou canst not say I did it: never shake

lines.text: Thy gory locks at me.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Gentlemen, rise: his highness is not well.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Sit, worthy friends: my lord is often thus,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: And hath been from his youth: pray you, keep seat;

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: The fit is momentary; upon a thought

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: He will again be well: if much you note him,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: You shall offend him and extend his passion:

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: MACBETH

lines.text: Which might appal the devil.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: O proper stuff!

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: This is the very painting of your fear:

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: A woman’s story at a winter’s fire,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Authorized by her grandam. Shame itself!

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Why do you make such faces? When all’s done,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: You look but on a stool.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Prithee, see there! behold! look! lo! how say you?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Why, what care I? If thou canst nod, speak too.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: If charnel-houses and our graves must send

lines.text: Shall be the maws of kites. Ghost vanishes.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: What, quite unmann’d in folly?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: If I stand here, I saw him.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Fie, for shame!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Blood hath been shed ere now, i’ the olden time,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Ere human statute purged the gentle weal;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Ay, and since too, murders have been perform’d

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Too terrible for the ear: the times have been,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: That, when the brains were out, the man would die,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And there an end; but now they rise again,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: With twenty mortal murders on their crowns,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And push us from our stools: this is more strange

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Than such a murder is.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: My worthy lord,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Your noble friends do lack you.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I do forget.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Do not muse at me, my most worthy friends;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I have a strange infirmity, which is nothing

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: To those that know me. Come, love and health to all;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Then I’ll sit down. Give me some wine; fill full.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I drink to the general joy o’ the whole table,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And to our dear friend Banquo, whom we miss;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Would he were here! to all, and him, we thirst,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And all to all.

lines.speaker: LORDS
lines.text: Our duties, and the pledge.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Avaunt! and quit my sight! let the earth hide thee!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Thy bones are marrowless, thy blood is cold;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Thou hast no speculation in those eyes

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Which thou dost glare with!

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Think of this, good peers,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: But as a thing of custom: ’tis no other;

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Only it spoils the pleasure of the time.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: What man dare, I dare:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Approach thou like the rugged Russian bear,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The arm’d rhinoceros, or the Hyrcan tiger;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Take any shape but that, and my firm nerves

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Shall never tremble: or be alive again,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And dare me to the desert with thy sword;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: If trembling I inhabit then, protest me

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The baby of a girl. Hence, horrible shadow!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Unreal mockery, hence! Ghost vanishes.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Why, so: being gone,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I am a man again. Pray you, sit still.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: You have displaced the mirth, broke the good meeting,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: With most admired disorder.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Can such things be,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And overcome us like a summer’s cloud,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Without our special wonder? You make me strange

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Even to the disposition that I owe,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: When now I think you can behold such sights,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And keep the natural ruby of your cheeks,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: When mine is blanch’d with fear.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What sights, my lord?

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: I pray you, speak not; he grows worse and worse;

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Question enrages him. At once, good night:

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Stand not upon the order of your going,

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: But go at once.

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Good night; and better health

lines.speaker: LENNOX
lines.text: Attend his majesty!

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: A kind good night to all!

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: It will have blood; they say, blood will have blood:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Stones have been known to move and trees to speak;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Augurs and understood relations have

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: By magot-pies and choughs and rooks brought forth

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: The secret’st man of blood. What is the night?

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Almost at odds with morning, which is which.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: How say’st thou, that Macduff denies his person

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: At our great bidding?

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: Did you send to him, sir?

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I hear it by the way; but I will send:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: There’s not a one of them but in his house

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: I keep a servant fee’d. I will to-morrow,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: And betimes I will, to the weird sisters:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: More shall they speak; for now I am bent to know,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: By the worst means, the worst. For mine own good,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: All causes shall give way: I am in blood

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Stepp’d in so far that, should I wade no more,

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Returning were as tedious as go o’er:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Strange things I have in head, that will to hand;

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Which must be acted ere they may be scann’d.

lines.speaker: LADY MACBETH
lines.text: You lack the season of all natures, sleep.

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Come, we’ll to sleep. My strange and self-abuse

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: Is the initiate fear that wants hard use:

lines.speaker: MACBETH
lines.text: We are yet but young in deed.

stage_directions.text: A banquet prepared. Enter Macbeth, Lady Macbeth, Ross, Lennox, Lords, and Attendants.
First Murderer appears at the door.
Exeunt all but Macbeth and Lady Macbeth.

generously cheerful

throne; chair of state

glosses.definition: whole as marble, firmly set as rock — Macbeth's fantasy of stability if Banquo had been killed

accuse of unfriendliness

glosses.phrase: Stand not upon the order of your going

murder will be answered with murder — the play's law of revenge

glosses.phrase: Stones have been known to move and trees to speak

annotations.title: The ghost and the politics of sight
annotations.body:
The banquet scene stages a double play. On one plane, Macbeth and the guests are performing a state dinner — toasts, seating, ceremony. On the other, Banquo's ghost enters and sits in Macbeth's chair, visible to Macbeth and to the audience, invisible to everyone else. Macbeth's performance collapses; the guests see only a king screaming at empty furniture.

Shakespeare's staging is precise. The ghost enters twice, exits twice; the intervals are the scene's rhythm. The first entrance destroys Macbeth's opening speech about Banquo's absence; the second destroys his forced toast to the missing guest. The timing is exact: the ghost answers each lie at the moment it is spoken.

The politics of sight rhymes with 2.1 (the dagger). Again Macbeth asks whether what he sees is real; again the play refuses to answer. Unlike the ghost in *Hamlet*, which other characters also see, Banquo's ghost is visible only to the man who ordered his murder. Whether ghost or hallucination, it performs the same function: forcing the truth onto a stage where every other actor is trying to maintain a lie.

annotations.title: 'In blood stepp'd in so far'
annotations.body:
After the guests have fled, Macbeth delivers the play's most famous metaphor for tragic acceleration: 'I am in blood / Stepp'd in so far, that, should I wade no more, / Returning were as tedious as go o'er.' The metaphor is of a man standing in a river of blood at its midpoint, calculating that turning back will cost the same as pushing forward.

The calculation is economic, not moral. He has not been transformed by the murders; he has simply concluded that the sunk cost makes continuation rational. This is the most chilling kind of evil: evil that persists because reversing it is inefficient. The same line of thinking governs the Macduff family murder in 4.2 — Macbeth kills Lady Macduff and the children not because it helps him, but because stopping would be as tedious as continuing.

The line is sometimes quoted as tragic lament. Read carefully, it is closer to tragic bookkeeping.

trials.prompt: Only Macbeth sees Banquo's ghost. The staging serves:
options: A politics of sight — the ghost forces truth onto a stage where every other actor is maintaining a lie

trials.prompt: 'In blood stepp'd in so far' is:
options: Tragic bookkeeping — sunk cost reasoning: turning back is as costly as pushing forward

trials.prompt: Lady Macbeth dismisses the ghost as:
options: A 'painted devil' — the kind of image that only frightens children; she insists he is hallucinating
